pool_brief: You are given a starter HTML file for a Nigerian business scenario. Write script.js to process and log the page data using conditional statements. Your script.js must: (1) use at least one if/else if/else chain with three or more branches and correct condition order (most specific first); (2) use at least one switch statement with three or more cases, a break after every case, and a default; (3) use at least one ternary operator to assign a short label or display value; (4) use the || default value pattern at least once to handle a potentially empty or falsy value; (5) combine && or || in at least one condition. Log all outputs with descriptive labels using console.log. Do not modify the HTML file.

project_pool.context: A PiggyVest savings dashboard where the user's tier (Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum) is determined by their balance, and their daily interest rate is determined by their tier.

files.content:
PiggyVest

Save better. Invest smarter.

Account Details

Ada Okonkwo

Balance: ₦87,500

Goal: ₦200,000

Saving streak: 21 days

Tier System

Bronze: below ₦50,000

Silver: ₦50,000 – ₦99,999

Gold: ₦100,000 – ₦199,999

Platinum: ₦200,000 and above

criteria.description: An if/else if/else chain with 4 branches assigns the correct tier: Platinum (>= 200000), Gold (>= 100000), Silver (>= 50000), Bronze (else). Balance is 87500 so Silver is the correct output. Conditions are ordered from highest to lowest (most specific first). At least one condition uses && or || — e.g. checking if streak >= 21 && tier is Silver for a milestone bonus

A switch on the tier string maps each tier to a daily interest rate: 'Platinum' → 0.5%, 'Gold' → 0.35%, 'Silver' → 0.25%, 'Bronze' → 0.1%; break after every case; default handles unknown tiers; the Silver rate (0.25%) is logged for balance 87500

At least one ternary assigns a short label (e.g. goalMet = balance >= goal ? 'Goal reached!' : 'Still saving'); || default pattern used for the empty nickname field (e.g. const displayName = nickname || ownerName); both outputs are logged with descriptive labels

camelCase naming; const for fixed values and computed results; no var; all console.log calls have descriptive labels; no unreachable conditions; correct numeric outputs (balance 87500 → Silver tier → 0.25% interest rate → daily interest = 87500 * 0.0025 = 218.75)

project_pool.context: A Konga marketplace page where shipping cost and delivery speed are determined by the order total and destination zone.

files.content:
Konga

Nigeria's Trusted Online Marketplace

Order Details

Emeka Nwosu

Order total: ₦65,000

Delivery zone: Abuja

Shipping Options

Free delivery on orders above ₦50,000 (Lagos only)

Express: same-day (Lagos), next-day (Abuja), 3 days (other zones)

Standard: 3 days (Lagos), 5 days (Abuja), 7 days (other zones)

criteria.description: An if/else if/else chain determines shipping cost based on order total: free if total >= 50000 AND zone is 'Lagos'; discounted (e.g. ₦500) if total >= 50000 but zone is not Lagos; standard fee (e.g. ₦1500) otherwise. Uses && to combine the total and zone conditions. Conditions in correct order (most specific first). 65000 and Abuja correctly produces discounted or standard fee (not free)

A switch on the delivery zone determines delivery days for standard shipping: 'Lagos' → '3 days', 'Abuja' → '5 days', any other → '7 days'. Break after every case. Default handles unrecognised zones. Abuja correctly produces '5 days'

At least one ternary used for a short label (e.g. freeDelivery = total >= 50000 && zone === 'Lagos' ? 'Free' : 'Paid'); || default pattern used for the empty promo-code field (e.g. const promoDisplay = promoCode || 'No promo code applied'); both logged with labels
camelCase; const for all fixed values; no var; all logs have descriptive labels; the zone condition in the if chain uses === (strict equality); outputs are correct for total 65000 and zone 'Abuja'

project_pool.context: A student progress page for a Publica Academy learner showing completion percentage, pace status, and next action recommendation.

files.content:
Publica Academy

Learn. Build. Grow.

Course Progress

Ngozi Eze

JavaScript Fundamentals

Modules completed: 11

Total modules: 18

Days enrolled: 28

criteria.description: An if/else if/else chain determines pace status based on completion percentage (modulesDone / totalModules * 100): >= 70% → 'On track — excellent pace'; >= 50% → 'On track — steady progress'; >= 30% → 'Slightly behind — pick up the pace'; below 30% → 'At risk — intensive review needed'. 11/18 = 61.1% so 'On track — steady progress' is correct. Conditions ordered most specific (highest %) first

A switch on a derived week number (e.g. Math.ceil(daysEnrolled / 7) or a category like 'early', 'mid', 'late') OR a switch on a pace label string maps to a specific next-action recommendation. At least 3 cases with break and a default. Output is logged with a label

At least one ternary used (e.g. const certificateEligible = modulesDone === totalModules ? 'Eligible' : 'In progress'); || default pattern used for the empty preferred-name field (e.g. const displayName = preferredName || studentName); both logged
camelCase; const for all values; no var; correct percentage calculation (11/18 * 100 ≈ 61.1); all logs labeled; correct pace output for the given data

project_pool.context: A Bolt ride summary page where the fare category (budget, standard, premium) and surge pricing status are determined based on fare amount, time of day, and rating.

files.content:
Bolt

Your city. Your ride.

Trip Details

Chidi Okafor

Fare: ₦3,200

Distance: 12.5 km

Hour of day: 8

Driver rating: 4.95

criteria.description: An if/else if/else chain assigns fare category based on fare amount: >= 5000 → 'Premium ride', >= 2000 → 'Standard ride', else → 'Budget ride'. Fare is 3200 so 'Standard ride' is correct. A combined condition check using && flags surge pricing: if (hour >= 7 && hour <= 9) or (hour >= 17 && hour <= 20) → log 'Surge pricing active'. hour is 8 so surge fires

A switch on the fare category string ('Premium ride', 'Standard ride', 'Budget ride') maps to a tip recommendation: Premium → 'Consider a ₦500 tip', Standard → 'Consider a ₦200 tip', Budget → 'No tip expected'. Break after each case. Default handles unexpected values. 'Standard ride' correctly logs the ₦200 tip suggestion

At least one ternary used (e.g. const driverBonus = driverRating >= 4.9 ? 'Bonus eligible' : 'Standard pay'); || default pattern used for the empty promo field (e.g. const promoDisplay = promoCode || 'No promo applied'); both logged with labels
camelCase; const used correctly; no var; all logs labeled; surge check correctly identifies hour 8 as peak (morning rush); all outputs correct for the given data (fare 3200, hour 8, rating 4.95)

project_pool.context: A Cowry Budget app page showing a user's monthly income, expenses by category, and spending health assessment.

files.content:
Cowry Budget

Track every Naira. Build every dream.

May 2025 — Spending Summary

Bayo Adeyemi

Income: ₦320,000

Rent: ₦90,000

Food: ₦42,000

Transport: ₦22,000

Entertainment: ₦18,000

Savings target: ₦80,000

criteria.description: An if/else if/else chain assesses spending health based on what percentage of income went to expenses. totalExpenses = 90000 + 42000 + 22000 + 18000 = 172000; expenseRate = 172000/320000*100 ≈ 53.75%. Chain: >= 80% → 'Critical overspend', >= 65% → 'High spending — review budget', >= 50% → 'Moderate — watch entertainment', else → 'Healthy spending'. 53.75% lands in 'Moderate'. Combined condition: if remaining >= savingsTarget && expenseRate < 60 → 'Savings goal achievable'. remaining = 320000 - 172000 = 148000 >= 80000 AND expenseRate < 60 → both true

A switch on a spending category string ('Critical overspend', 'High spending — review budget', 'Moderate — watch entertainment', 'Healthy spending') recommends a specific action. Break after every case. Default handles unexpected values. 'Moderate — watch entertainment' logs the correct recommendation

At least one ternary used (e.g. const savingsStatus = remaining >= savingsTarget ? 'On track' : 'Short of target'); || default pattern used for the empty budget-name field (e.g. const budgetLabel = budgetName || 'May Budget'); both logged with descriptive labels
camelCase; const for all values and computed results; no var; all arithmetic correct (totalExpenses = 172000, remaining = 148000, expenseRate ≈ 53.75%); all console.log calls have descriptive labels
